key_themes: Strong comparable sales growth and revenue beats
Analyst upgrades with raised price targets to ~$1,100
Market outperformance (up ~17% YTD vs S&P decline)
Resilient membership model, digital sales, and fuel traffic
Premium valuation as a defensive 'safety' stock
summary: Costco has posted strong Q2 FY2026 results with net sales up 9.1% to $68.24 billion, comparable sales growth of ~7%, digitally-enabled sales over 20%, and EPS rising to $4.58. Shares are up approximately 16-17% in 2026 while the S&P 500 has declined, supported by loyal membership, fuel discounts driving traffic amid high gas prices, and consistent execution.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/why-costco-stock-16-2026-143428381.html)[[2]](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/04/11/costco-stock-screaming-buy-april-1-thing-happens/)[[3]](https://investor.costco.com/news/news-details/2026/Costco-Wholesale-Corporation-Reports-Second-Quarter-and-Year-to-Date-Operating-Results-for-Fiscal-2026-and-February-Sales-Results/default.aspx) Analysts have responded with multiple upgrades and PT raises toward $1,100 (Outperform ratings), though high valuations (~50x forward earnings) remain a noted caution. Investor and social media sentiment is solidly bullish on its recession-resistant qualities despite some broader consumer spending concerns.
sources: Q2 FY2026 earnings report (9.1% sales growth, EPS +14%), March sales data (~11% YoY growth), 8+ news articles (Yahoo Finance, Motley Fool, investor relations), 20+ recent X posts (analyst notes, sales reactions), Mizuho/JPM price target increases
